req.title: Close encounter (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for Encounter closure requirements.

Requirements tested:
1. A clinician shall close an Encounter.
2. Closed Encounters shall reject further modifications.
3. Closed Encounters SHALL accept addendum Observations.
4. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (end-to-end).
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, call, patch

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain / application layer stubs
# (Replace these imports with your real modules.)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from myapp.audit import AuditEventBus  # async emit(event) -> None
from myapp.encounters import EncounterService  # business logic
from myapp.exceptions import (
    EncounterAlreadyClosedError,
    EncounterNotFoundError,
    InsufficientPrivilegesError,
    ModificationRejectedError,
)
from myapp.models import (
    AuditEvent,
    Encounter,
    EncounterStatus,
    Observation,
    ObservationType,
    Practitioner,
)
from myapp.repositories import EncounterRepository, ObservationRepository

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers / factories
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICIAN_ID = "practitioner-001"
NON_CLINICIAN_ID = "patient-999"
ENCOUNTER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
PATIENT_ID = "patient-001"


def make_clinician(role: str = "clinician") -> Practitioner:
    return Practitioner(id=CLINICIAN_ID, role=role, name="Dr. Ada Lovelace")


def make_encounter(
    status: EncounterStatus = EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
    encounter_id: str = ENCOUNTER_ID,
) -> Encounter:
    return Encounter(
        id=encounter_id,
        patient_id=PATIENT_ID,
        status=status,
        created_by=CLINICIAN_ID,
        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )


def make_observation(
    obs_type: ObservationType = ObservationType.STANDARD,
    encounter_id: str = ENCOUNTER_ID,
) -> Observation:
    return Observation(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        encounter_id=encounter_id,
        clinician_id=CLINICIAN_ID,
        obs_type=obs_type,
        value="Blood pressure: 120/80",
        recorded_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


@pytest.fixture()
def encounter_repo() -> MagicMock:
    repo = MagicMock(spec=EncounterRepository)
    repo.get_by_id = AsyncMock(return_value=make_encounter())
    repo.save = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda enc: enc)
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def observation_repo() -> MagicMock:
    repo = MagicMock(spec=ObservationRepository)
    repo.save = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda obs: obs)
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def audit_bus() -> MagicMock:
    bus = MagicMock(spec=AuditEventBus)
    bus.emit = AsyncMock()
    return bus


@pytest.fixture()
def service(
    encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    observation_repo: MagicMock,
    audit_bus: MagicMock,
) -> EncounterService:
    return EncounterService(
        encounter_repo=encounter_repo,
        observation_repo=observation_repo,
        audit_bus=audit_bus,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def clinician() -> Practitioner:
    return make_clinician(role="clinician")


@pytest.fixture()
def non_clinician() -> Practitioner:
    return make_clinician(role="receptionist")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Utility: assert a single AuditEvent was emitted with expected attributes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _assert_audit_emitted(
    audit_bus: MagicMock,
    *,
    action: str,
    resource_type: str,
    resource_id: str,
    agent_id: str,
    outcome: str = "success",
) -> AuditEvent:
    """Assert emit() was called and return the captured AuditEvent."""
    audit_bus.emit.assert_called_once()
    emitted: AuditEvent = audit_bus.emit.call_args[0][0]
    assert isinstance(emitted, AuditEvent), "Emitted object must be an AuditEvent"
    assert emitted.action == action, f"Expected action={action!r}, got {emitted.action!r}"
    assert emitted.resource_type == resource_type
    assert emitted.resource_id == resource_id
    assert emitted.agent_id == agent_id
    assert emitted.outcome == outcome
    assert emitted.timestamp is not None, "AuditEvent must have a timestamp"
    return emitted


def _assert_audit_call_count(audit_bus: MagicMock, count: int) -> None:
    assert audit_bus.emit.call_count == count, (
        f"Expected {count} AuditEvent(s), got {audit_bus.emit.call_count}"
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1.  CLOSING AN ENCOUNTER
# ===========================================================================


class TestCloseEncounter:
    """A clinician shall be able to close an Encounter."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_clinician_can_close_open_encounter(
        self, service: EncounterService, clinician: Practitioner, encounter_repo: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        encounter = make_encounter(status=EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = encounter

        closed = await service.close_encounter(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician
        )

        assert closed.status == EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        assert closed.closed_by == clinician.id
        assert closed.closed_at is not None

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_closed_encounter_is_persisted(
        self, service: EncounterService, clinician: Practitioner, encounter_repo: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        encounter_repo.save.assert_called_once()
        saved: Encounter = encounter_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.status == EncounterStatus.CLOSED

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_closing_nonexistent_encounter_raises(
        self, service: EncounterService, clinician: Practitioner, encounter_repo: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.side_effect = EncounterNotFoundError(ENCOUNTER_ID)

        with pytest.raises(EncounterNotFoundError):
            await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_non_clinician_cannot_close_encounter(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        non_clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientPrivilegesError):
            await service.close_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=non_clinician
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_closing_already_closed_encounter_raises(
        self, service: EncounterService, clinician: Practitioner, encounter_repo: MagicMock
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(EncounterAlreadyClosedError):
            await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

    # --- Audit ---

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_emitted_on_close(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="close",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=clinician.id,
            outcome="success",
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_emitted_even_on_failure(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """AuditEvent with outcome=failure must still be emitted on error."""
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(EncounterAlreadyClosedError):
            await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="close",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=clinician.id,
            outcome="failure",
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()
        before = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        after = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        emitted: AuditEvent = audit_bus.emit.call_args[0][0]
        assert before <= emitted.timestamp <= after


# ===========================================================================
# 2.  REJECTING MODIFICATIONS ON CLOSED ENCOUNTERS
# ===========================================================================


class TestClosedEncounterRejectsModifications:
    """Closed Encounters shall reject further modifications."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_update_encounter_rejected_when_closed(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.update_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=clinician,
                updates={"reason": "Updated reason"},
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_add_standard_observation_rejected_when_closed(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        obs = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.STANDARD)

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.add_observation(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=obs
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_delete_observation_rejected_when_closed(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.delete_observation(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=clinician,
                observation_id="obs-001",
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_reopen_encounter_rejected_when_closed(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """Re-opening is itself a modification and must be rejected."""
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.reopen_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_no_persistence_on_rejected_modification(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.update_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=clinician,
                updates={"reason": "Should not persist"},
            )

        encounter_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    # --- Audit on rejected modifications ---

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_emitted_on_rejected_modification(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.update_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=clinician,
                updates={"reason": "Illegal update"},
            )

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="update",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=clinician.id,
            outcome="failure",
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_emitted_on_rejected_standard_observation(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        obs = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.STANDARD)

        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.add_observation(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=obs
            )

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="add_observation",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=clinician.id,
            outcome="failure",
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 3.  ADDENDUM OBSERVATIONS ARE ALLOWED ON CLOSED ENCOUNTERS
# ===========================================================================


class TestAddendumObservations:
    """Closed Encounters SHALL accept addendum Observations."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_addendum_observation_accepted_on_closed_encounter(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        result = await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        assert result.obs_type == ObservationType.ADDENDUM
        observation_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_addendum_observation_is_linked_to_encounter(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        saved: Observation = observation_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.encounter_id == ENCOUNTER_ID

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_addendum_observation_records_clinician(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        saved: Observation = observation_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved.clinician_id == clinician.id

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_encounter_status_remains_closed_after_addendum(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """Adding an addendum must NOT change the Encounter status."""
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        # Encounter.save should NOT have been called (status unchanged)
        encounter_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_non_clinician_cannot_add_addendum(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        non_clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientPrivilegesError):
            await service.add_observation(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=non_clinician,
                observation=addendum,
            )

    # --- Audit on addendum ---

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_emitted_on_successful_addendum(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="add_observation",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=clinician.id,
            outcome="success",
        )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_references_observation_id(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)
        observation_repo.save.return_value = addendum

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        emitted: AuditEvent = audit_bus.emit.call_args[0][0]
        assert emitted.detail.get("observation_id") == addendum.id

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_emitted_on_failed_addendum_by_non_clinician(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        non_clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        with pytest.raises(InsufficientPrivilegesError):
            await service.add_observation(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=non_clinician,
                observation=addendum,
            )

        _assert_audit_emitted(
            audit_bus,
            action="add_observation",
            resource_type="Encounter",
            resource_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
            agent_id=non_clinician.id,
            outcome="failure",
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 4.  END-TO-END AUDIT EVENT COVERAGE
# ===========================================================================


class TestAuditEventEndToEnd:
    """Every operation must produce an AuditEvent — comprehensive E2E checks."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_exactly_one_audit_event_per_close_operation(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        _assert_audit_call_count(audit_bus, 1)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_exactly_one_audit_event_per_addendum_operation(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter(
            status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED
        )
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)

        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        _assert_audit_call_count(audit_bus, 1)

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_events_across_full_lifecycle(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        observation_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Full lifecycle:
          1. Add a standard observation to an open encounter  → 1 AuditEvent
          2. Close the encounter                              → 1 AuditEvent
          3. Attempt an illegal update                        → 1 AuditEvent (failure)
          4. Add an addendum observation                      → 1 AuditEvent
        Total: 4 AuditEvents, in order.
        """
        open_encounter = make_encounter(status=EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        closed_encounter = make_encounter(status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED)

        # Step 1 – standard observation on open encounter
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_encounter
        std_obs = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.STANDARD)
        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=std_obs
        )

        # Step 2 – close encounter
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_encounter
        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        # Step 3 – illegal modification on closed encounter
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = closed_encounter
        with pytest.raises(ModificationRejectedError):
            await service.update_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID,
                clinician=clinician,
                updates={"reason": "Nope"},
            )

        # Step 4 – valid addendum
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = closed_encounter
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)
        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        _assert_audit_call_count(audit_bus, 4)

        calls: list[call] = audit_bus.emit.call_args_list
        actions = [c[0][0].action for c in calls]
        outcomes = [c[0][0].outcome for c in calls]

        assert actions == ["add_observation", "close", "update", "add_observation"]
        assert outcomes == ["success", "success", "failure", "success"]

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_schema_completeness(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """Every emitted AuditEvent must carry all required fields."""
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        emitted: AuditEvent = audit_bus.emit.call_args[0][0]
        required_fields = {
            "id",
            "action",
            "resource_type",
            "resource_id",
            "agent_id",
            "outcome",
            "timestamp",
        }
        missing = required_fields - set(vars(emitted).keys())
        assert not missing, f"AuditEvent is missing fields: {missing}"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_event_ids_are_unique(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """Each AuditEvent must have a unique identifier."""
        open_encounter = make_encounter(status=EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
        closed_encounter = make_encounter(status=EncounterStatus.CLOSED)

        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = open_encounter
        await service.close_encounter(encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician)

        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = closed_encounter
        addendum = make_observation(obs_type=ObservationType.ADDENDUM)
        await service.add_observation(
            encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician, observation=addendum
        )

        ids = [c[0][0].id for c in audit_bus.emit.call_args_list]
        assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "AuditEvent IDs must be unique"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_audit_bus_failure_does_not_suppress_business_result(
        self,
        service: EncounterService,
        clinician: Practitioner,
        encounter_repo: MagicMock,
        audit_bus: MagicMock,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Even if the AuditEventBus is unavailable, the primary operation should
        either succeed (with a compensating retry) or propagate the audit error
        explicitly — it must NOT silently lose the audit trail.

        This test verifies the service does NOT swallow the audit error silently.
        """
        audit_bus.emit.side_effect = RuntimeError("Audit bus unavailable")
        encounter_repo.get_by_id.return_value = make_encounter()

        # The service must surface the audit failure (not silently ignore it).
        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="Audit bus unavailable"):
            await service.close_encounter(
                encounter_id=ENCOUNTER_ID, clinician=clinician
            )
```

---

## Design decisions and coverage rationale

| Area | What is tested | Why |
|---|---|---|
| **Close Encounter** | Happy path, persistence, not-found, wrong role, already-closed | All branches of the close operation |
| **Rejection of modifications** | `update_encounter`, `add_observation` (standard), `delete_observation`, `reopen_encounter`; also checks `repo.save` is never called | Ensures the guard is present for every mutation surface |
| **Addendum Observations** | Accept on closed encounter, link to encounter, record clinician, status unchanged, non-clinician rejected | Distinguishes the one legal exception from all illegal ones |
| **AuditEvent – per operation** | Exactly one event per call; failure events on errors; events on rejected modifications | The requirement says *every* operation |
| **AuditEvent – schema** | All required fields present; unique IDs; correct `action`/`outcome` values; timestamp in range |

2. Closed Encounters shall reject further modifications.
3. Closed Encounters SHALL accept addendum Observations.
4. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (end-to-end).
"""

def make_encounter(

) -> Encounter:

Requirements tested:
1. A clinician shall close an Encounter.
2. Closed Encounters shall reject further modifications.
3. Closed Encounters SHALL accept addendum Observations.
4. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation (end-to-end).
"""

import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Any
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, call, patch

import pytest
import pytest_asyncio

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain / application layer stubs

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from myapp.audit import AuditEventBus  # async emit(event) -> None
from myapp.encounters import EncounterService  # business logic
from myapp.exceptions import (
    EncounterAlreadyClosedError,
    EncounterNotFoundError,
    InsufficientPrivilegesError,
    ModificationRejectedError,
)
from myapp.models import (
    AuditEvent,
    Encounter,
    EncounterStatus,
    Observation,
    ObservationType,
    Practitioner,

from myapp.repositories import EncounterRepository, ObservationRepository

# Helpers / factories

CLINICIAN_ID = "practitioner-001"
NON_CLINICIAN_ID = "patient-999"
ENCOUNTER_ID = str(uuid.uuid4())
PATIENT_ID = "patient-001"


def make_clinician(role: str = "clinician") -> Practitioner:
    return Practitioner(id=CLINICIAN_ID, role=role, name="Dr. Ada Lovelace")


def make_encounter(
    status: EncounterStatus = EncounterStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
    encounter_id: str = ENCOUNTER_ID,
) -> Encounter:
    return Encounter(
        id=encounter_id,
        patient_id=PATIENT_ID,
        status=status,
        created_by=CLINICIAN_ID,
        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )


def make_observation(
    obs_type: ObservationType = ObservationType.STANDARD,
    encounter_id: str = ENCOUNTER_ID,
) -> Observation:
    return Observation(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        encounter_id=encounter_id,
        clinician_id=CLINICIAN_ID,
        obs_type=obs_type,
        value="Blood pressure: 120/80",
        recorded_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
    )

@pytest.fixture()
def encounter_repo() -> MagicMock:
    repo = MagicMock(spec=EncounterRepository)
    repo.get_by_id = AsyncMock(return_value=make_encounter())
    repo.save = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda enc: enc)
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()

    repo = MagicMock(spec=ObservationRepository)
    repo.save = AsyncMock(side_effect=lambda obs: obs)

    return bus

def service(

    observation_repo: MagicMock,

) -> EncounterService:

        encounter_repo=encounter_repo,
        observation_repo=observation_repo,
        audit_bus=audit_bus,
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def clinician() -> Practitioner:
    return make_clinician(role="clinician")


@pytest.fixture()
def non_clinician() -> Practitioner:
    return make_clinician(role="receptionist")
